meta.title: Mercedes-Benz OM621/OM622/OM626 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz OM621/OM622/OM626 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes OM621/OM622/OM626 are legendary pre-chamber diesels — known for extreme longevity and simplicity. Backed by UK specialist consensus and classic car community insight [MB-INTERVIEW].

rows.value: Confidence: 90% confidence — based on decades of owner data and specialist knowledge of these classic diesel engines.

rows.metric: Most failures are cheap, well‑understood repairs (injector pumps, glow plugs)

rows.value: Good availability through classic specialists, but some parts are becoming scarce

rows.metric: Reconditioned cost well under 30% of classic vehicle values

rows.value: Sum of the five rows above

rows.metric: One-line verdict: The OM621/OM622/OM626 are legendary for their endurance and simplicity — injection pump and glow plug issues are common but easy to resolve — buy on rust-free shell, not engine condition.
rows.value: Best for: Classic Mercedes enthusiasts wanting bulletproof mechanical diesels.

rows.metric: Avoid if: You want modern power, refinement, or a fast diesel.

The OM621, OM622, and OM626 are Mercedes-Benz's earliest mass-produced four-cylinder diesel engines. Introduced in 1958 with the OM621, they powered some of the most iconic diesel Mercedes models ever built. The OM622 was a transitional upgrade, and the OM626 carried the torch into the late 1980s.

rows.value: * Fitted to the W110, W114, W115, and W123 models

rows.metric: * Legendary for its ability to run on almost any diesel fuel
rows.value: * Known as the "barrel" engine for its robust, cast-iron construction

rows.metric: * Found primarily in the W115/W123 models

rows.value: * 2.0L/2.4L pre-chamber diesel with improved fuel injection

rows.metric: * Fitted to the W123 and W201 (190D)
rows.value: * The final iteration of this legendary engine family

rows.metric: * Mechanical simplicity — no electronics, easy to repair
rows.value: * Fuel flexibility — can run on various diesel blends

rows.metric: * Rust-free example is more important than engine condition
* Injection pump wear — can cause starting issues and poor running
rows.value: * Glow plug failure — cold starting can be problematic

rows.metric: * Fuel system air leaks — from perished rubber lines
rows.value: * Cylinder head cracks — between valves on high-mileage examples

rows.metric: * Oil leaks — from gaskets and seals (common with age)
These engines are almost universally respected for their ability to survive decades of service with simple maintenance. They are not fast, but they are faithful and extremely long-lasting.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an OM621/OM622/OM626 typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].

cta.label: → Compare OM621/OM622/OM626 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: How much does an OM621/OM622/OM626 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned OM-series diesel typically costs £1,200–£2,500 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by displacement and variant. Fitting adds £300–£600 depending on labour rates and vehicle model.

items.question: Is the OM621/OM622/OM626 a reliable engine?
items.answer: Yes — these engines rate 4.5★ out of 5 based on decades of owner data [MB-INTERVIEW]. They are legendary for their extreme longevity, with many examples exceeding 300,000 miles when maintained.

items.question: What is the most common OM621/OM622/OM626 failure?
items.answer: Injection pump wear is the most common issue, causing starting problems. Glow plug failure and fuel system air leaks are also frequent on older examples.

items.question: Which Mercedes models use the OM621/OM622/OM626?
items.answer: These engines power the W110 (190D), W114/W115 (Stroke 8, 200D/220D/240D), W123 (200D/220D/240D), and W201 (190D, 190D 2.5) — classic Mercedes from 1958 to 1988.

items.question: How long does the OM621/OM622/OM626 last?
items.answer: With simple maintenance, these engines routinely exceed 300,000 miles. Many specialists report examples at 500,000+ with original internals — one of the most durable engines ever built [MB-INTERVIEW].
